title: On macos/m1 podman can't detect arch correctly
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

On MacOS/m1 fresh installation of podman and init considers it's running on x86, therefore fails


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
brew upgrade
brew install podman
podman machine init
podman machine start
```
```
❯ grep bin/qemu ~/.config/containers/podman/machine/qemu/podman-machine-default.json
  "/usr/local/bin/qemu-system-x86_64",
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
❯ podman machine init
Extracting compressed file
Image resized.
Machine init complete
To start your machine run:
	podman machine start

❯ podman machine start
Starting machine "podman-machine-default"
Waiting for VM ...
Error: qemu exited unexpectedly with exit code -1, stderr: qemu-system-x86_64: Unknown Error
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Error: failed to connect: dial tcp [::1]:52129: connect: connection refused
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
Error: failed to connect: dial tcp [::1]:52129: connect: connection refused
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman` or `brew info podman`):**

```
❯ brew info podman
==> podman: stable 4.3.1 (bottled), HEAD
Tool for managing OCI containers and pods
https://podman.io/
/usr/local/Cellar/podman/4.3.1 (185 files, 48MB) *
  Poured from bottle on 2022-11-30 at 11:35:05
From: https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/blob/HEAD/Formula/podman.rb
License: Apache-2.0 and GPL-3.0-or-later
==> Dependencies
Build: go-md2man ✔, go@1.18 ✘
Required: qemu ✔
==> Options
--HEAD
	Install HEAD version
==> Caveats
Bash completion has been installed to:
  /usr/local/etc/bash_completion.d

To restart podman after an upgrade:
  brew services restart podman
Or, if you don't want/need a background service you can just run:
  /usr/local/opt/podman/bin/podman system service --time=0
==> Analytics
install: 30,322 (30 days), 80,079 (90 days), 238,731 (365 days)
install-on-request: 27,806 (30 days), 75,737 (90 days), 233,374 (365 days)
build-error: 24 (30 days)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked [the Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)?**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
- latest Macos Ventura 13.0.1

comments.body: This sounds like you installed the x86 podman version, please check your podman install.

> This sounds like you installed the x86 podman version, please check your podman install.

I used official documentation, however I also tried to install one from official website via podman-desktop sw, it also got the same issue. The podman itself seems to work well, but it downloads x86 VM image at the end
Did you by any chance have your brew installation migrated (restored from backup as well) from an Intel Mac? In this case homebrew could continue to operate as if it was running on x86 mac.

> Did you by any chance have your brew installation migrated (restored from backup as well) from an Intel Mac? In this case homebrew could continue to operate as if it was running on x86 mac.

I migrated my MacOS from x86 macbook, but then I had to remove brew and reinstall from scratch

> I migrated my MacOS from x86 macbook, but then I had to remove brew and reinstall from scratch

From blog posts and installer script it seems that arm64 version of brew is installed side by side with amd64, so, your env might still point to amd64 brew after installation of arm64 version. https://blog.smittytone.net/2021/02/07/how-to-migrate-to-native-homebrew-on-an-m1-mac/

Could you share the output of `which brew`?
@arixmkii you are likely on the right track  thanks for the help here.

> which brew
```
❯ which brew
/usr/local/bin/brew

❯ ls -l /usr/local/bin/brew
lrwxr-xr-x  1 xxx  admin  28 Aug 23  2018 /usr/local/bin/brew -> /usr/local/Homebrew/bin/brew

❯ ls -l /usr/local/Homebrew/bin/brew*
-rwxr-xr-x  1 xxx  admin  4606 Nov 30 11:14 /usr/local/Homebrew/bin/brew
```

@emalovan for arm64 brew it should be
```
% which brew
/opt/homebrew/bin/brew
```

Please check your environment to point to the correct installation of brew. You can verify this location in brew installer sources (pointing to arm64 section here) https://github.com/Homebrew/install/blob/master/install.sh#L129
this might be it! thanks for you help guys!
